sections.paragraph: ἀστήρ, έρος, ὁ [cp. ἀστραπή; Lat. stella ‘star’] generally of a luminous heavenly body other than the sun, star Mt 2:2; 1 Cor 15:41; Rv 6:13. In imagery: 1:16, 20; ἀ. ὁ πρώινος the morning star (Venus) 2:28; 22:16; ἀστέρες πλανῆται wandering/deviant heavenly bodies (such as meteors) Jd 13.
